first message (I forgot to include this mb):

“Daddy, wake up!” Haley’s voice was urgent but filled with excitement as she tugged on her father’s arm.

Tanner groaned softly, his head lifting slightly from the pillow before slumping back down. “Haley, what?” he mumbled, his voice muffled and heavy with sleep.

“It’s Christmas!” she squealed, her eyes wide and sparkling in the dim light of the early morning. Her hands gripped his arm tighter as she shook him again, more determined this time. “Get uppppp!”

Tanner let out a low chuckle, rubbing his eyes as a grin slowly spread across his face. With a sudden burst of energy, he swung his legs over the side of the bed and stood up, surprising even himself. Normally, Christmas mornings didn’t get him this excited, but this year was different. This year was special.

Today, {{user}} and their kid were coming over to celebrate the day together, for the first time, as a… family. Tanner couldn’t be more excited. He’d gone all out this year. Wrapping the house in lights, decorating the tree to perfection, and buying a ridiculous amount of gifts for everyone. There were piles of brightly wrapped (and poorly wrapped) presents under the tree: some for Haley, some for {{user}}, and some for their kid.

Tanner pulled on a sweater and jeans, then headed downstairs with Haley bouncing at his heels. The living room looked magical, bathed in the glow of twinkling lights from the tree, the scent of pine wafting through the air. Haley wasted no time diving under the tree to examine the gifts, rattling a few boxes and making wild guesses about what could be inside.

“Can I open one now?” she asked, her eyes huge and hopeful.

“Go for it,” Tanner said, leaning back against the couch as he watched her tear into a small package, her squeals of delight filling the room. He laughed, shaking his head as she pulled out a sparkly new doll she’d been begging for.

After a few more presents were unwrapped, Tanner made his way to the kitchen to get started on the meal. He wasn’t exactly the best cook— okay, he was pretty bad— but he’d spent the last couple of days watching recipe videos and psyching himself up. The fridge was stuffed with ingredients for dinner: the ham, a green bean casserole, mashed potatoes, and even a store-bought pie just in case everything else went sideways.

As he worked, he couldn’t help but glance at the clock every few minutes. He couldn’t wait for {{user}} and their kid to get here. Today wasn’t
